# Tools available
- read(path, line_range?): read a file; output tags each line with an 8-hex content-hash prefix for hash-anchored edits.
- write(path, content): overwrite a file (atomic fsync).
- edit(path, edits?, inserts?): hash-anchored edit — reference hashes from the last read.
- bash(command, cwd?, timeout_ms?): run a shell command (YOLO default; denylist applied).
- grep(pattern, path?, flags?): ripgrep-style search.
- find(path, name?, type?): filesystem find.
- ls(path, long?, all?): list a directory.
- web_fetch(url, timeout_ms?): HTTP GET → markdown. Output capped at ~40K chars (~10K tokens) with head+tail truncation — for research, prefer reading web_search snippets directly; only web_fetch when you need structured content a snippet can't convey. Gated by per-profile allowlist PLUS recent-web_search-URL bypass.
- web_search(query, max_results?): search the open web via local SearxNG at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Returns {title, url, snippet, engine}[] with upstream-engine fallback. Use before web_fetch to look up current info / latest versions / docs.
- memory(command, path, ...): read and write notes that persist across sessions. Two scopes via path prefix: /memories/project/... for repo-specific knowledge (project conventions, hard-won discoveries, dead-ends), /memories/global/... for cross-project preferences. Six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename. Check what's there with view /memories/project before non-trivial work; write notes ONLY when a discovery would help a future session — load-bearing facts (API quirks, conventions, counter-intuitive findings), not narrative.

6cfacd46  // Plan 04-03 Task 2 — D-23 harness-side hot-swap composition.

5796e667  // the harness-side state tied to the OLD profile must be re-initialized so
b4f550be  // the NEXT user turn picks up the NEW profile without restarting the pi

9a103e00  //         expose a public addSpanProcessor/removeSpanProcessor mutator. We
5cc5aaa6  //         avoid rebuilding the whole SDK by making the processor's profile
e7dbad20  //         ref mutable (swapSpanProcessor). See
ad0cd935  //         emmy-telemetry/src/profile-stamp-processor.ts for rationale.
a2c23396  //
3d3bbcac  //   (3) web_fetch allowlist re-audit
18a2117c  //       — The allowlist in the OLD profile is replaced with the NEW
3a10b7d9  //         profile's harness.tools.web_fetch.allowlist via setInitialAudit.
e68f47c3  //         This is the Plan 03-06 D-27 mechanism: bindBadge sets the module-
05f960e3  //         level state; setInitialAudit reseeds it with the new state.
a2c23396  //
af62bd7d  // D-23 invariants (from 04-RESEARCH §5.3):
abe4be70  //   - pi's AgentSession transcript is NOT touched here (preserved for free
67d84179  //     by pi — MCP subprocess registry + SessionManager transcript + tools
b3f1bb29  //     registry all survive provider swap).
e5f08bf7  //   - D-06 in-flight-turn guard in the /profile handler prevents this path
147cdec3  //     from firing mid-generation; an open OTel span cannot be mid-flight.
e3b0c442  
112a041e  import { loadProfile } from "./profile-loader";
e0cbdab3  import {
09a78a97  	runBootOfflineAudit,
885c3390  	setInitialAudit,
d0f81295  } from "./offline-badge";
3b71deb2  import type { ProfileSnapshot } from "./types";
e0cbdab3  import {
458d3718  	type EmmyProfileStampProcessor,
fab49acf  	type EmmyToolRegistration,
8c63f9c5  	swapSpanProcessor,
769a38ef  } from "@emmy/telemetry";
e3b0c442  
09559435  export interface HarnessSwapHandles {
10fccfaf  	/**
5de73b76  	 * Set the current profile on whatever closure/module owns the live
aa72bce0  	 * ProfileSnapshot reference (pi-emmy-extension.ts factory closure).
f48539e2  	 */
f15bb4a6  	replaceProfileRef: (snap: ProfileSnapshot) => void;
10fccfaf  	/**
dee47042  	 * The currently-installed EmmyProfileStampProcessor instance. Its profile
561df44a  	 * ref is mutated in place — no SDK rebuild required.
f48539e2  	 */
674f8bc3  	profileStampProcessor: EmmyProfileStampProcessor;
10fccfaf  	/**
7461214a  	 * Return the current tool registrations so harness-swap can re-audit
cc8e5637  	 * against the NEW profile's allowlist (WR-04 fix — previously the badge
f295857c  	 * was hardcoded to green after swap, masking posture regressions when
cbeb1c56  	 * swapping to a stricter-allowlist profile). When absent, falls back to
5c8c9f33  	 * forcing green (pre-fix behavior) — callers should always provide this
a7b1b16e  	 * for production code paths; test doubles may omit it.
f48539e2  	 */
4025b8d5  	getToolRegistrations?: () => readonly EmmyToolRegistration[];
d10b36aa  }
e3b0c442  
9c21467a  export interface ReloadHarnessProfileResult {
3bbaed6e  	snap: ProfileSnapshot;
d10b36aa  }
e3b0c442  
8d64a30d  /**
7423e390   * Perform the three-part D-23 hot-swap after the engine-layer swap
6909b1e6   * succeeded. Resolves with the freshly-loaded ProfileSnapshot once all
134027ff   * three re-inits are done.
64e75266   *
f7a8168d   * Caller should invoke this ONLY on orchestrator exit code 0; exit 5 leaves
a3370e47   * the OLD engine running (no harness-side change needed); exit 6 means the
148c54a0   * rollback path already re-targeted the prior profile, so the harness
0ef62916   * state does not move either. See registerProfileCommand handler in
a6dddfb9   * slash-commands.ts for the exit-code routing.
533a9f11   */
0ce6b53c  export async function reloadHarnessProfile(
b1f08d90  	newDir: string,
6f44b11f  	handles: HarnessSwapHandles,
8c0fe097  ): Promise<ReloadHarnessProfileResult> {
cd1f66e2  	// (1) Load the new profile bundle → ProfileSnapshot.
d1cda031  	const snap = await loadProfile(newDir);
e3b0c442  
a3c8899f  	// (2) Swap the closure-captured reference so before_provider_request +
aa6f76c2  	//     compaction trigger + slash-command handlers see the new profile.
50cb4ff8  	handles.replaceProfileRef(snap);
e3b0c442  
72195fb6  	// (3) Hot-swap the OTel stamp processor's internal profile ref. Next span
f67c641f  	//     started after this call stamps the new emmy.profile.* attrs. This
d89fa370  	//     uses swapSpanProcessor (mutable-in-place) because the pinned OTel
44bc7737  	//     SDK does NOT expose addSpanProcessor/removeSpanProcessor publicly.
fa126ed5  	swapSpanProcessor(handles.profileStampProcessor, {
04ec0071  		id: snap.ref.id,
a42575f4  		version: snap.ref.version,
df4ef8ba  		hash: snap.ref.hash,
eeb33785  	});
e3b0c442  
87651cb7  	// (4) web_fetch allowlist re-audit. The new profile's allowlist governs
1dcdde5c  	//     future tool.web_fetch enforcement. Absent allowlist → empty array.
a2277fe5  	//
8bc8783c  	//     WR-04 FIX: previously we hardcoded `badge_state: "green"` here,
23251c53  	//     which masked posture regressions when the new profile had a
de063766  	//     stricter allowlist than the tools actually required. Now we run
1f70e9ed  	//     the same runBootOfflineAudit the boot path runs — it correctly
4acd4c7f  	//     flips the badge to red if any registered tool's required_hosts
74772497  	//     aren't covered by the new allowlist.
3d7cf45e  	const newAllowlist = snap.harness.tools.web_fetch?.allowlist ?? [];
7cf81c50  	if (handles.getToolRegistrations) {
8a29abe0  		const toolRegistrations = handles.getToolRegistrations();
1888d452  		const auditResult = runBootOfflineAudit({
1aa851f2  			toolRegistrations,
f971462b  			allowlist: newAllowlist,
2d92f989  			// Swallow the stderr banner on swap — the /profile handler already
556c90d8  			// notifies the user of the swap outcome; a second "[emmy] OFFLINE
e5c5fbfa  			// OK" line on top would be noise. The audit RESULT still propagates.
40459d69  			stderr: () => {},
206aa438  		});
bc368838  		setInitialAudit(auditResult);
ad65360e  	} else {
74f01193  		// Legacy / test-double path: no registrations available, so preserve
f854399d  		// pre-WR-04 behavior (force green). Callers in production MUST supply
66283fd3  		// getToolRegistrations to get the real audit.
ff324c73  		setInitialAudit({
d163c0ee  			offline_ok: true,
a031cdc2  			violating_tool: null,
ce18e825  			violating_host: null,
7bb3783c  			badge_state: "green",
206aa438  		});
a32a3bb7  	}
e9d19b7d  	// Mark the new allowlist as applied so downstream readers can observe
1e675f8f  	// the swap in tests without chasing enforcement-context internals.
335573da  	_lastReloadAllowlist = [...newAllowlist];
e3b0c442  
6f2d06fa  	return { snap };
d10b36aa  }
e3b0c442  
215b3a90  // Test introspection: record the most recently applied allowlist so unit
d0f6e75f  // tests can assert it propagated through reloadHarnessProfile. Not part of
13da4b8c  // the public production API.
cad56eb1  let _lastReloadAllowlist: string[] = [];
e3b0c442  
6ec6e785  /** Test-only: read the most recent allowlist passed through reloadHarnessProfile. */
01f6535d  export function __getLastReloadAllowlistForTests(): readonly string[] {
cdd14ffc  	return _lastReloadAllowlist;
d10b36aa  }
e3b0c442  
44111aab  /** Test-only: clear the recorded allowlist between tests. */
64e4bacb  export function __resetLastReloadAllowlistForTests(): void {
25cce84a  	_lastReloadAllowlist = [];
d10b36aa  }

## File: `/data/projects/emmy/emmy_serve/profile/schema.py`

This is the single file that defines **all** Pydantic v2 schemas for the profile bundle system, including `harness.yaml`, `serving.yaml`, and `profile.yaml`.

### How the optional `sampling_defaults` block is wired

There are actually **two** distinct `sampling_defaults` concepts in this codebase, and they live at different layers:

#### 1. `SamplingDefaults` — serving.yaml (base profile)

Defined at line ~127 of `schema.py`:

```python
class SamplingDefaults(BaseModel):
    """Prior-sampling settings; harness per-tool sampling overrides these."""
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)
    temperature: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=2.0)
    top_p: float = Field(gt=0.0, le=1.0)
    top_k: int = Field(gt=0)
    repetition_penalty: float = Field(gt=0.0)
    max_tokens: int = Field(gt=0)
    stop: list[str] = []
```

This is a **required** field on `ServingConfig` (the root of `serving.yaml`). It establishes the base sampling defaults that every variant inherits.

#### 2. `VariantSamplingDefaults` — harness.yaml (optional, variant-level override)

Defined at line ~312 of `schema.py`:

```python
class VariantSamplingDefaults(BaseModel):
    """Phase 4 HARNESS-08 — variant-level sampling_defaults."""
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)
    temperature: Optional[float] = Field(default=None, ge=0.0, le=2.0)
    top_p: Optional[float] = Field(default=None, gt=0.0, le=1.0)
    top_k: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, gt=0)
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, gt=0)
    repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = Field(default=None, gt=0.0)
```

This is the **optional** block on `HarnessConfig` (the root of `harness.yaml`):

```python
class HarnessConfig(BaseModel):
    """Root of harness.yaml — Phase-2 placeholder values allowed as long as they type-check."""
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid", frozen=True)
    prompts: PromptsConfig
    context: ContextConfig
    tools: ToolsConfig
    agent_loop: AgentLoopConfig
    advanced_settings_whitelist: list[str] = []
    sampling_defaults: Optional[VariantSamplingDefaults] = None   # ← optional!
    chat_template_kwargs: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
    subagents: Optional[SubagentsConfig] = None
```

Key design decisions:

- **All fields are `Optional`** — a variant may only diverge on a subset (e.g., `v3.1-reason` overrides only `temperature` + `top_p`). Absent fields fall through to the base `serving.yaml` `SamplingDefaults`.
- **`extra="forbid", frozen=True`** — typo safety + immutability, consistent with every BaseModel in the schema.

#### 3. Runtime consumption: `before-request-hook.ts`

The wiring from schema to wire happens in `packages/emmy-provider/src/before-request-hook.ts` (lines ~90–108). On every LLM request, the `handleBeforeProviderRequest` hook receives an optional `variantSnapshot`:

```typescript
if (variantSnapshot) {
    const sd = variantSnapshot.harness.sampling_defaults;
    if (sd) {
        if (typeof sd.temperature === "number") payload.temperature = sd.temperature;
        if (typeof sd.top_p === "number") payload.top_p = sd.top_p;
        if (typeof sd.top_k === "number") payload.top_k = sd.top_k;
        if (typeof sd.max_tokens === "number") payload.max_tokens = sd.max_tokens;
    }
    // ... also merges chat_template_kwargs
}
```

The `variantSnapshot` comes from the TypeScript `VariantSnapshot` interface in `packages/emmy-provider/src/types.ts`, which mirrors the Python schema's `VariantSamplingDefaults` shape as a plain `harness.sampling_defaults` object with optional `temperature`, `top_p`, `top_k`, `max_tokens` fields.

#### 4. The variant profile mechanism

Sibling variants (e.g., `v3.1-default`, `v3.1-reason`, `v3.1-precise`) share a **byte-identical** `serving.yaml` (enforced by CI). The only per-variant differences live in `harness.yaml` — specifically `sampling_defaults`, `chat_template_kwargs`, `per_tool_sampling`, and `prompts`. This means you can route different turns to different variants (e.g., "plan" turns → `v3.1-reason` with `temperature=0.6`, "edit" turns → `v3.1-default` with `temperature=0.0`) **without restarting the vLLM engine**.

#### 4. The variant profile mechanism

Sibling variants (e.g., `v3.1-default`, `v3.1-reason`, `v3.1-precise`) share a **byte-identical** `serving.yaml` (enforced by CI). The only per-variant differences live in `harness.yaml` — specifically `sampling_defaults`, `chat_template_kwargs`, `per_tool_sampling`, and `prompts`. This means you can route different turns to different variants (e.g., "plan" turns → `v3.1-reason` with `temperature=0.6`, "edit" turns → `v3.1-default` with `temperature=0.0`) **without restarting the vLLM engine**.
